title: RL-005 Phase 1.5+2 Execution Plan — Plugin Security State Refactor
consumer_purpose: Unify parallel hooks[]/registrations[] arrays into plugins: PluginRegistration[], eliminate TOCTOU in trigger(), fix bus subscriber staleness, and switch capability-denial logging to Effect.logWarning per RL-005 revision 3
claim_atoms: State.hooks[] and State.registrations[] unified into State.plugins: PluginRegistration[] via zipping at end of init()
PluginRegistration extended with hooks: Hooks and pluginName: string fields (capabilities remain in CapabilityRegistry)
Trigger() snapshots plugins at top of call to prevent TOCTOU (RL-005 G1)
Bus subscriber reads InstanceState.get(state) per event instead of closure capture (RL-005 Phase 1.5)
Capability denials logged via yield* Effect.logWarning in generator context (RL-005 G1 pattern)
All downstream consumers remain compatible — Interface type unchanged
Gates 2 (tool namespace) and 3 (config stripSecrets) confirmed fully implemented — zero changes needed

already_done.phase_1_capability_infrastructure: capability/types.ts, enforcer.ts, registry.ts, hook-map.ts, hook-dispatch-guard.ts, index.ts all exist and functional
already_done.gate_2_tool_namespace: registry.ts:420-430 has capability check + plugin-{sanitized}-{id} prefix

gaps_this_plan_fixes.phase_1_5_state_refactor: State still has parallel hooks[] + registrations[] arrays
gaps_this_plan_fixes.toctou_snapshot: Trigger reads s.hooks live, no local snapshot
gaps_this_plan_fixes.bus_subscriber_staleness: Captures hooks/registrations by closure at line 361
gaps_this_plan_fixes.effect_log_warning: Uses log.warn instead of Effect.logWarning for capability denials

files_modified.changes: 11 edits: type definitions (PluginRegistration, State), zipping step in init(), 6 consumer rewrites (trigger, list, config-hook, dispose, bus-subscriber, return statement)

files_not_modified: packages/opencode/src/session/*.ts — Interface unchanged, all consumers compatible
new_files: packages/opencode/test/plugin/capability-enforcement.test.ts — RL-005 bisect tests for TOCTOU, bus subscriber, Effect.logWarning

edits.description: Extend PluginRegistration with hooks and pluginName
Replace parallel arrays with unified plugins array in State

edits.lines: ~220-230 (init function, after all plugins loaded, before config hooks)
edits.description: Add zipping step: zip hooks[] and registrations[] into plugins[] after loading completes
edits.before: (no zipping step — hooks and registrations used directly)

edits.description: Config hook filter: iterate plugins instead of hooks[i]/registrations[i]
edits.after:
for (const reg of plugins) {
  const pluginId = reg.pluginId
edits.rl005_reference: Gate 3: config exposure filtering (already implemented, refactored for unified state)

edits.description: Dispose finalizer: iterate plugins instead of hooks
edits.after:
Effect.forEach(plugins, (reg) =>
  Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => Promise.resolve(reg.hooks.dispose?.()), catch: (error) => { log.error("plugin dispose hook failed", { pluginId: reg.pluginId, error }) } })

edits.description: Bus subscriber: read InstanceState.get(state) per event instead of closure capture
edits.before: Effect.forEach(hooks.map((_, i) => i), (i) => { const fn = hooks[i]["event"]; const pluginId = registrations[i]?.pluginId; ...
edits.after: Effect.gen(function* () { const s = yield* InstanceState.get(state); const currentPlugins = s.plugins; for (const reg of currentPlugins) { const fn = reg.hooks["event"]; ...

edits.description: Trigger: TOCTOU snapshot (const plugins = s.plugins at top) + Effect.logWarning for denials
edits.rl005_reference: Gate 1: TOCTOU fix + Effect.logWarning for capability denials

edits.description: List function: map plugins instead of hooks[i]/registrations[i]
edits.before: return s.hooks.map((hook, i) => { const listed: ListedPlugin = Object.assign(hook, { pluginId: s.registrations[i]?.pluginId }); return listed })
edits.after: return s.plugins.map((reg) => { const listed: ListedPlugin = Object.assign(reg.hooks, { pluginId: reg.pluginId }); return listed })

edits.description: Return statement: return plugins instead of hooks, registrations

design_decisions.topic: Zipping approach (keep loading logic, zip at end)
design_decisions.decision: Preserve local hooks[] and registrations[] accumulators during plugin loading; zip into plugins[] at end. applyPlugin() signature unchanged. This minimizes diff surface and avoids touching the delicate plugin loading pipeline.
alternatives_rejected: Change applyPlugin to push PluginRegistration directly — requires rewriting internal/external plugin registration code, higher risk
Store hooks directly on PluginRegistration during loading — requires changing internalPlugins() return type

design_decisions.topic: Capabilities stay in CapabilityRegistry (not on PluginRegistration)
design_decisions.decision: RL-005 designed PluginRegistration to include capabilities: ResolvedCapability[]. However, the current codebase already has a working CapabilityRegistry + HookDispatchGuard pattern. Storing capabilities on PluginRegistration would create a dual source of truth and require rewriting the guard system. The registry-backed approach works correctly and is simpler to maintain.
alternatives_rejected: Add capabilities to PluginRegistration and use inline hasCapability() — would break HookDispatchGuard, require rewriting capability checks in trigger, bus subscriber, registry.ts

design_decisions.topic: Effect.logWarning in generator, log.error in catch handlers
design_decisions.decision: Use yield* Effect.logWarning(...) (generator context) for capability denials in the trigger. Use log.error(...) (regular logger) in tryPromise catch handlers (non-generator context). This is correct because Effect.logWarning creates an Effect that must be yielded to execute; calling it without yield* in a catch handler would silently discard the log.
design_decisions.rl005_reference: RL-005 specifies Effect.logWarning + Effect.logError; adapted for context correctness

design_decisions.decision: list() currently mutates hooks objects via Object.assign(hook, { pluginId }). This is the existing behavior and changing it could break consumers that rely on pluginId being stamped onto the hooks object. We preserve the pattern with plugins: Object.assign(reg.hooks, { pluginId: reg.pluginId }).
alternatives_rejected: Return new objects without mutation — would break consumers that expect pluginId on the hooks reference

risks.description: Zipping creates stale reference: all plugins loaded before zipping, no dynamic post-init loading — safe
Object.assign mutation in list() corrupts hooks: existing behavior preserved
Bus subscriber uses InstanceState.get(state) — always gets latest plugins, no stale closure risk
capabilityRegistry reference unaffected — separate from plugins array
dispatchGuard uses pluginId string — works with reg.pluginId from unified array

downstream_impact.note: All consumers access the Plugin service through the Interface type — trigger(), list(), checkCapability(). The Interface signature is unchanged. Only the internal State representation changes.

step_2_existing_tests.expected: all 14 existing plugin test suites pass: trigger.test.ts, auth-override.test.ts, loader-shared.test.ts, capability-proxy-bisect.test.ts, cloudflare.test.ts, codex.test.ts, github-copilot-models.test.ts, install-concurrency.test.ts, install.test.ts, openai-rollout.test.ts, openai-ws.test.ts, shared.test.ts, workspace-adapter.test.ts, xai.test.ts

test_cases: TOCTOU snapshot — trigger reads consistent snapshot even if plugins array mutates mid-iteration
Effect.logWarning on denial — capability denial produces Effect.logWarning with pluginId, hook, requiredCapability
bus subscriber freshness — event subscriber sees plugins added after init via InstanceState.get(state)
state refactor — State.plugins is Array<{pluginId, trust, hooks, pluginName}>
stripSecrets per-plugin regression — config exposure filtering still works (Gate 3)
tool namespace regression — plugin tool gets namespace prefix (Gate 2)

rollback_plan: Revert edits E9 through E1 in reverse order. Each edit is independently reversible because the zipping step (E3) creates a local plugins variable — the original hooks and registrations arrays remain in local scope.
deferred_to_separate_lanes: Phase 1 capability manifest schema validation (capability/schema.ts, loader.ts, install.ts)

tests: trigger reads snapshot at call time, not live reference — verify plugin added after trigger starts but before iteration is not dispatched
external plugin without session.hooks capability gets denial log when chat.message triggers
denial log includes pluginId, hook name, requiredCapability fields
built-in plugin bypasses capability check (no denial log)
event subscriber reads InstanceState.get(state) per event and sees newly added plugins
event subscriber handles plugins with no event hook (skips gracefully)
State.plugins is Array<PluginRegistration> with pluginId, trust, hooks, pluginName fields
PluginRegistration.hooks is a Hooks object (not null/undefined)
list() returns Hooks[] with pluginId stamped via Object.assign
plugin tool registered with namespace prefix plugin-{sanitized}-{id}
plugin without tool.register capability gets tool registration denied
plugin with secrets.access receives full config including API keys
plugin without secrets.access receives config with secrets stripped
